focus: Security review of renamed deep-agent-improvement scripts and command YAML shell/path handling

findingDetails.title: Unquoted workflow placeholders allow shell/path injection before script validation
findingDetails.evidence: User-controlled spec_folder and target_path placeholders are interpolated unquoted into auto and confirm workflow shell commands before Node scripts can parse or normalize paths.
findingDetails.recommendation: Route user fields through argv arrays or a quoting/escaping helper and enforce canonical spec_folder and .opencode/agents/*.md path normalization before constructing shell commands.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same unquoted placeholder pattern appears in both auto and confirm workflow command surfaces; reviewed path-consuming scripts parse argv after shell interpolation.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 security finding in a previously unreviewed dimension; no refinements-only findings counted.
edgeCases: Dispatcher may escape placeholders out-of-band; no guarantee was present in reviewed packet, so severity remains P1 with downgrade trigger.
Runtime command-dispatch implementation was not expanded beyond declared representative files.
ruledOut: No active P0 because exploitability depends on command dispatcher escaping behavior.
No separate finding for broad SKILL.md allowed tools because packet-local writes and Bash are expected for this workflow.
